title: Multi-step plan executor with rollback
description: Executes a sequence of interdependent steps (e.g., git pull → build → test → deploy) with automatic rollback on any step failure, logging each step's output and state.
motivation: Users frequently ask me to perform complex multi-step operations (deployments, migrations, refactors) where a single failure can corrupt state. Currently I must manually orchestrate each step and handle errors ad-hoc, leading to brittle scripts and unrecoverable states.
implementationApproach: Build a pipeline executor that wraps each step in a try/except block, captures stdout/stderr, and on failure runs a pre-defined rollback function (e.g., git revert, restore backup) before reporting the error with full context.

title: Context-aware file patcher with semantic diff
description: Applies code changes by understanding the surrounding context (function boundaries, indentation, imports) rather than just line numbers, and generates a semantic diff showing what changed and why.
motivation: Current str_replace/edit_file tools are fragile: they fail when line numbers shift after edits, or when whitespace differs. Users report 'patch failed' errors that require manual intervention. A semantic patcher would understand code structure and adapt to minor variations.
implementationApproach: Parse the target file into an AST (using tree-sitter or language-specific parser), locate the target code block by signature (function name, class name, pattern match), apply the change, and regenerate the file with proper formatting. Show a human-readable diff with change reasons.

title: Autonomous web form filler with visual verification
description: Combines browser automation with screenshot analysis to fill and submit web forms: extracts form fields via DOM, fills them using context from user instructions, takes a screenshot before submit, and asks user to confirm or correct.
motivation: Users often ask me to 'fill out this form' or 'submit this application' but current browser tools lack form awareness. I frequently fill wrong fields, miss required inputs, or submit without validation. This capability would make web automation reliable and safe.
implementationApproach: Use browser_extract to get all input/select/textarea elements with labels and placeholders, map them to user-provided data, fill using browser_type, take a screenshot via browser_screenshot, analyze with analyze_image to verify correctness, then submit only after user confirmation.

title: Self-healing multi-step workflow executor
description: Executes complex multi-step workflows (e.g., deploy, migrate, refactor) with automatic rollback on failure, checkpoint/restore, and self-healing retry logic that adapts to transient errors.
motivation: Users frequently ask me to perform interdependent operations where a single failure corrupts state. Current ad-hoc orchestration leads to brittle scripts, unrecoverable states, and manual cleanup. This combines git_operations, sandbox_execute, and self_heal to make workflows resilient.
implementationApproach: Wrap existing tool calls in a state machine that snapshots state before each step, catches failures, and either rolls back to last good checkpoint or retries with adaptive delays. Integrate with self_heal for automatic environment fixes.

title: Visual form interaction agent
description: Autonomously fills and submits web forms by combining DOM analysis, visual verification via screenshots, and user confirmation before submission. Handles dynamic forms, validation errors, and multi-page flows.
motivation: Current browser tools lack form awareness—I frequently fill wrong fields, miss required inputs, or submit without validation. Users report frustration when I 'break' forms. This makes web automation reliable and safe by adding visual confirmation and error recovery.
implementationApproach: Use browser_extract to map form fields, then browser_type to fill with context-aware values. Before submit, take a screenshot and use analyze_image to verify field contents. Ask user to confirm or correct via ask_human. On validation errors, retry with corrected input.

title: Context-aware semantic code patcher
description: Applies code changes by understanding surrounding context (function boundaries, indentation, imports) rather than exact line numbers. Generates a semantic diff explaining what changed and why, and adapts to minor whitespace/variation differences.
motivation: Current str_replace/edit_file tools fail when line numbers shift after edits or whitespace differs. Users report 'patch failed' errors requiring manual intervention. A semantic patcher would understand code structure and gracefully handle variations, making code modifications reliable.
implementationApproach: Parse the target file into an AST or use tree-sitter to identify structural boundaries. When applying a change, search for the target context semantically (ignoring whitespace, matching function signatures). Generate a structured diff with explanations. Fall back to line-based patching if semantic fails.
